title: lac-lsp — Language Server
problem: Editor integrations and external tools need a persistent, indexed view of the workspace's features — not a fresh filesystem scan on every request. Without a language server, every client (VS Code extension, web app, CI) re-walks the repo independently, leading to duplicate work and stale data.
analysis: A dual-protocol server: LSP over stdio for editor clients, and a REST + SSE HTTP API on localhost:7474 for browser, CI, and web app clients. Maintains an in-memory feature index rebuilt from the workspace on startup, kept live via chokidar file watching. Supports three startup modes — LSP-only, HTTP-only, or both. The HTTP API exposes endpoints for listing features, reading/writing individual features, blame, lint, and a Server-Sent Events stream for real-time change notifications.
implementation: LacLspServer.ts wires together the LSP connection (vscode-languageserver SDK over stdio), the HTTP server (Fastify or Express), and the FeatureIndex. FeatureIndex.ts maintains an in-memory map of all features, rebuilt on start and patched on file change events from chokidar. LSP handlers in src/lsp/handlers/ implement codeLens, hover, diagnostics, definition, and workspace symbols. HttpServer.ts registers REST routes and the SSE /events endpoint. src/lib/lint.ts and src/lib/blame.ts power the lint and blame endpoints. Mode is selected via CLI args at startup.

decisions.rationale: Editor clients speak LSP; browser and CI clients speak HTTP. Running both from one process shares the same feature index and file watcher, avoiding duplication.
alternativesConsidered: HTTP only — misses deep editor integration (diagnostics, symbols, code lenses)

decisions.decision: In-memory index with chokidar file watching
decisions.rationale: Feature sets are small (hundreds of files at most). In-memory gives sub-millisecond queries. File watching keeps it live without polling.
alternativesConsidered: SQLite — persistent but adds a file dependency and migration complexity
No index, always scan — too slow for frequent editor requests

decisions.decision: SSE for real-time updates over WebSockets
decisions.rationale: SSE is unidirectional and simpler to implement and proxy. The use case (broadcast feature changes to listeners) is inherently server-to-client.
alternativesConsidered: WebSockets — bidirectional, more complex, not needed here

knownLimitations: In-memory index is lost on crash — all clients must handle reconnection and re-query
HTTP CORS allows wildcard origin by default — security risk in shared network environments
No authentication or authorization on any endpoint
SSE late subscribers miss all events prior to connection — no event replay
LSP and HTTP share a process — if one subsystem crashes it takes the other down
successCriteria: `lac-lsp --http-only --workspace /path/to/repo` starts, responds to GET /health with a feature count, and GET /features returns all feature.json files found in the workspace.

lineage.spawnReason: The language server is the shared index that powers both the VS Code extension and the web app's live data.
